title: Guarea guara
intro: Guarea guara, also known as Guarea tree, is a species of plant in the family Meliaceae, native to the tropical regions of South America. The Guarea tree has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Guarea guara include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made from Guarea guara include decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Guarea guara belongs to the family Meliaceae. This family is commonly known as the Mahogany family. The genus Guarea is a group of flowering plants native to tropical Africa and Central America. It contains around 100 species of trees, some of which are used for their timber or medicinal properties. Guarea guara is a species within this genus, specifically found in tropical regions of Central and South America.
